problem_statement: How to reliably evaluate the quality of code produced by human-in-the-loop LLM software development agents (HULA) deployed at Atlassian, given that functional-correctness unit testing is costly and LLM-based scoring is variable.
insufficiency_evidence: "LLM-based evaluations often produce fluctuating outcomes, limiting the ability to reliably assess small (e.g., 1–2%) incremental improvements in HULA's capabilities."
solution_mechanisms: human-in-the-loop agent with review/refine at each step
score aggregation to mitigate LLM fluctuation
call for stable deterministic evaluation framework for HITL agents
